title: F5 BIG-IP LTM SSL/TLS Handshake Failures at the Edge
dataSources: F5 BIG-IP syslog with SSL error logging enabled, `sourcetype=f5:bigip:ltm:ssl:error`
app: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP (`Splunk_TA_f5-bigip`, Splunkbase 2680)
spl:
index=network sourcetype="f5:bigip:ltm:ssl:error" earliest=-24h
| stats count by src, dest, ssl_protocol, ssl_cipher
| where count > 50
| sort -count
| head 30
description: Spikes in SSL or TLS handshake failures at the BIG-IP often precede cipher mismatch rollouts, broken certificate chains, client compatibility issues, or TLS downgrade and interception attempts. Tracking failures by client source, protocol, and cipher supports rapid triage of application outages and suspicious clients.
value: Reduces mean time to detect misconfigured VIP certificates or cipher suites and highlights client populations that may be impacted by TLS policy changes.
implementation: Enable F5 syslog forwarding for LTM SSL handshake failure events per the add-on documentation. Install `Splunk_TA_f5-bigip` on search heads and indexers for field extractions. Baseline normal failure counts per VIP; alert when a source IP or destination VIP exceeds a rolling threshold. Correlate with certificate inventory and recent ASM or LTM policy changes.
visualization: Time chart (handshake failures), Table (top src by count), Pie chart (TLS version mix).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP — Source types
